--- 
title: "Design and Analysis of Experiments"
author: "Larry Winner"
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: 
    df_print: paged
geometry: left=2cm,right=2cm,top=2cm,bottom=2cm
documentclass: book
bibliography:
- book1.bib
- packages.bib
description: This is a course on Experimental Design and Analysis.
link-citations: yes
github-repo: rstudio/bookdown-demo
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
---

# Preface {-}

These notes are for STA 4211 at the University of Florida. All examples are based on published articles, though most data has been generated to match summary statistics.

Some commonly used notation used throughout the course notes with respect to random variables and estimators is as follows.

$$E\left\{\bullet\right\} - \mbox{ Expected Value or Expectation} $$

$$V\left\{\bullet\right\} - \mbox{ Variance} $$   
$$SE\left\{\bullet\right\} - \mbox{ Standard Error (square root of Variance)} $$
$$\mbox{COV}\left\{\bullet,\bullet\right\} - \mbox{ Covariance}$$
$$\hat{V}\left\{\bullet\right\} - \mbox{ Estimated Variance} $$
$$\hat{SE}\left\{\bullet\right\} - \mbox{ Estimated Standard Error} $$
$$\hat{\mbox{COV}}\left\{\bullet, \bullet\right\} - \mbox{ Estimated Covariance}$$

For **quantiles** of distributions used for constructing Confidence Intervals
or setting up rejection regions for tests, the probability will be the 
**lower tail** probability. Some common examples are given here.

$$
z_{.025}=-1.96 \qquad  z_{.975}=1.96 \qquad \chi_{.95;1}^2=3.84\qquad \chi_{.95;2}^2=5.99
$$
